title: betula pubescens subsp. minor
intro: Betula pubescens subsp. minor, also known as dwarf birch, is a subspecies of birch tree native to northern Europe and Asia, characterized by its short stature and broad leaves. The medicinal properties of dwarf birch include its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory problems, and provide relief from skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Dwarf birch is often prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, which can be used to treat its various health benefits and medicinal properties.
